title: SOC 2 Risk Assessment — Change-Induced Emergency Pattern Monitoring

dataSources: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, type, risk, start_date, end_date, state, short_description, u_emergency_flag)
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-90d state="Closed"
| eval emergency=coalesce(u_emergency_flag, if(match(type,"(?i)emergency"),"true","false"))
| where emergency="true"
| eval duration_h=(strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")-strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/3600
| stats count as emergency_changes, median(duration_h) as median_duration by risk, cmdb_ci
| sort - emergency_changes
description: CC3 expects risk identification and analysis, including changes that significantly affect the system. Correlating production emergency changes with recent deployments highlights process breakdowns where velocity outpaces risk assessment.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Risk Assessment — Change-Induced Emergency Pattern Monitoring. SOC 2 is how we show we run our services responsibly, and we keep evidence that supports that story.
implementation: (1) Ensure `u_emergency_flag` or `type` differentiates emergencies; (2) join `cmdb_ci` to service tier lookup; (3) alert when `emergency_changes` spikes vs baseline; (4) require post-implementation review field completeness; (5) feed results into quarterly risk committee deck. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, type, risk, start_date, end_date, state, short_description, u_emergency_flag).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure `u_emergency_flag` or `type` differentiates emergencies; (2) join `cmdb_ci` to service tier lookup; (3) alert when `emergency_changes` spikes vs baseline; (4) require post-implementation review field completeness; (5) feed results into quarterly risk committee deck.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-90d state="Closed"
| eval emergency=coalesce(u_emergency_flag, if(match(type,"(?i)emergency"),"true","false"))
| where emergency="true"
| eval duration_h=(strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")-strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/3600
| stats count as emergency_changes, median(duration_h) as median_duration by risk, cmdb_ci
| sort - emergency_changes
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 Risk Assessment — Change-Induced Emergency Pattern Monitoring (CC3.2, CC3.3)** — CC3 expects risk identification and analysis, including changes that significantly affect the system. Correlating production emergency changes with recent deployments highlights process breakdowns where velocity outpaces risk assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, type, risk, start_date, end_date, state, short_description, u_emergency_flag). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:change_request", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **emergency** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where emergency="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **duration_h** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by risk, cmdb_ci** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (emergency_changes by service), Table (risk, median_duration), Timechart (weekly emergency volume).
visualization: Bar chart (emergency_changes by service), Table (risk, median_duration), Timechart (weekly emergency volume).
knownFalsePositives: Evidence freshness lags during quarterly cycles, control owner transitions, or platform changes.
